commodityFP: Deliver operational grid-connected electrical energy storage assets as turnkey capital projects with single-source engineering, procurement, construction, and grid-code certification
unspscContext: custom_product_group: Battery Energy Storage System EPC Services (no UNSPSC ≥80% confidence)
fpExtensions: Storage medium neutrality — flow batteries, flywheels/CAES, thermal-to-power, hybrid H2-BESS
Grid-connected capital-asset EPC methodology — PV EPC, HPC charging hubs, PV+Wind hybrids, industrial microgrids, behind-the-meter electrification

candidates.title: Other Electric Power Generation
candidates.rationale: Literal standard NAICS for standalone BESS IPPs. Customer exists to operate grid-connected storage. Every Mein Solar differentiator (single-source EPC, VDE-AR-N 4110, FGW TR8) maps directly. Only friction: pure-play BESS IPPs often have in-house EPC talent.

candidates.title: Solar Electric Power Generation
candidates.rationale: PV+BESS co-location is industry-standard. Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency is a direct structural differentiator here — one grid connection, one certification dossier, one EMS, one warranty wrapper. Same VDE-AR-N 4110, same customer persona (Solarpark developers), same DSO interface.

candidates.title: Wind Electric Power Generation
candidates.rationale: Wind parks increasingly co-deploy BESS for curtailment capture and ancillary-service prequal. Same grid code, same certification path. Customer persona shifts to wind developers (Enercon, WPD, Energiequelle) — adjacent not identical. Mein Solar lacks wind-specific site heritage but EPC methodology translates cleanly.

candidates.title: Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings (except Miniwarehouses)
candidates.rationale: C&I landlords (logistics parks, office campuses, retail centers) deploying behind-the-meter BESS for peak-shaving Netzentgelte reduction and tenant resilience. Same MV interface, same Baugenehmigung process. Perfect match for single-source-EPC promise because landlords hate multi-vendor coordination.

candidates.title: Other Gasoline Stations and Fuel Dealers (incl. EV charging retail)
candidates.marketName: HPC Charging Hubs with BESS Buffer
candidates.rationale: Explicit Mein Solar differentiator (EV-Charging Buffer Configuration feature). HPC hubs (Ionity, EnBW mobility+, Fastned, Aral pulse) face weak rural MV grids; a BESS buffer enables 350 kW × 6+ dispensers on a 400 kVA feeder. FP-extension axis: HPC charging park EPC. Whitespace opportunity with rapidly inflecting adoption.

candidates.title: Electric Power Distribution
candidates.rationale: DSO-owned storage for grid constraint relief, voltage support, and deferred substation upgrade. Customer is regulated utility (Bayernwerk, Netze BW, Stromnetz Hamburg) — longer procurement cycles, EU-tender rules, stricter interoperability (IEC 61850). Same MV scale as Mein Solar reference; utility segment is slower but contracts are larger and predictable.

candidates.title: Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services
candidates.rationale: Hyperscalers and colocation operators (NTT, Digital Realty, Vantage, Equinix, CyrusOne, STACK) replacing lead-acid UPS with Li-ion BESS and stacking grid-services revenue. Same MV scale, same VDE-AR-N 4110 regime, but mission-critical availability (99.999%) and extreme fire-protection scrutiny. Strong growth — DE data-center capacity doubling 2025–2030.

candidates.title: Refrigerated Warehousing and Storage
candidates.rationale: Cold storage (Nordfrost, Kühne+Nagel, Frigoscandia) carries 24/7 high-kW refrigeration load. BESS enables peak-shaving and tariff arbitrage plus spoilage-outage resilience. Same MV interface, same C&I grid code, same site type as industrial landlord. Customer awareness low — education-led sale.

candidates.title: Industrial Gas Manufacturing
candidates.marketName: Green-H₂ Electrolyzer Sites with BESS Firming
candidates.rationale: Green-hydrogen projects (Linde, Air Liquide, Iberdrola, RWE green-H2) pair electrolyzers with BESS for curtailment capture, grid-forming support, and ramp-rate smoothing. Same grid code, same MV coupling, but EMS must be co-optimized with the electrolyzer stack — new control logic. Mein Solar's integration discipline transfers; H2-control layer is new skill.

candidates.title: Port and Harbor Operations
candidates.marketName: Port Electrification — Shore Power, Crane, Ferry Charging
candidates.rationale: Port electrification: shore power/OPS (2030 EU mandate), reach-stacker charging, ferry charging, crane smoothing. Customers are port authorities (HHLA Hamburg, BLG Bremerhaven, Duisport). Salt-air coastal environment requires IP upgrade. Grid scale matches Mein Solar reference. Public procurement + EU port-modernization subsidies.

candidates.title: Electric Bulk Power Transmission and Control
candidates.rationale: TSO-scale storage (50 Hertz, TenneT, Amprion, TransnetBW) at 100–500 MW — an order of magnitude above Mein Solar's 4.5 MW reference. Different grid code (VDE-AR-N 4120 HV), different certification (Type A HV plant cert), EU-tender-driven. Reachable only via consortium bid or subcontractor role.

candidates.title: Semiconductor and Related Device Manufacturing
candidates.rationale: Fabs (Infineon Dresden, GlobalFoundries, Bosch SiC, Intel Magdeburg) buy power-quality + ride-through solutions. BESS supplements dynamic UPS/flywheels for sag ride-through (200 ms events). Fab spec language (ITIC curve, SEMI F47) is bespoke; custom engineering per site. Entry difficult without fab reference.

candidates.title: Other Support Activities for Air Transportation
candidates.marketName: Airport Electrification — GSE, eTaxi, eVTOL Readiness
candidates.rationale: Airport GSE charging, eTaxi tractor charging, eVTOL vertiport readiness. Ownership split between airport operator and handling agents complicates sale. Apron-adjacent environment is safety-critical with aviation-side clearance requirements. Long decision cycles.

candidates.title: Sewage Treatment Facilities
candidates.rationale: Municipal WWTPs with peak pumping loads + co-location with biogas CHP create FCR participation and demand charge reduction opportunity. Customer is municipality — slow procurement, GWB-regulated tenders, no revenue-stacking culture. Niche adjacent to Mein Solar's core.

candidates.title: Other Food Crops Grown Under Cover
candidates.marketName: Electrified Greenhouse Operations with BESS Smoothing
candidates.rationale: Commercial greenhouses (tomato, herb, Dutch model) with grow-light + heat-pump loads. Electrification shifts demand profile to peaks that BESS can smooth. Customer persona is agricultural (not Mein Solar's traditional C&I), different sales channel. Demonstration-project fit only.

excludedMarkets.description: Residential rooftop PV (Mein Solar incumbent, below EPC scale)
Small commercial PV <500 kW (current market, below BESS EPC minimum)
Pure cell manufacturing 26111711 (upstream component, not customer)
327310 Cement Manufacturing (Tier-1 cut — kiln electrification pre-pilot)
517312 Wireless Telecommunications Carriers (Tier-1 cut — typical site <100 kWh, below P2 minimum)
